methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring allergen levels using specific kits and immunoassays to determine the presence and severity of allergic reactions in patients.

steps.description: The t-tIgE levels were measured using the Allerwatch® Tear IgE kit with immunochromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: measure t-tIgE levels using Allerwatch Tear IgE kit

steps.description: The s-sIgE antibody levels against 39 allergens were measured using the View Allergy 39® kit with a fluorescence enzyme immunoassay (FEIA).
steps.searchable_summary: measure s-sIgE levels using View Allergy 39 kit

steps.description: View Allergy 39® kit utilizes immunochromatography based on a sandwich method with β-galactosidase as the labeled substance.
steps.searchable_summary: process s-sIgE levels using sandwich method with β-galactosidase

steps.description: Judgment criteria of View Allergy 39® kit are applied to classify results into classes 1 to 6 based on index values.
steps.searchable_summary: classify s-sIgE results using View Allergy 39 criteria

steps.description: The t-sIgE level was measured using FEIA: IgE-RIST (radio-immunosorbent test).

methods.description: This method outlines the ethical considerations, participant selection criteria, and study registration details for a retrospective observational study on allergen testing.

steps.description: The study followed the ethical principles of the Declaration of Helsinki and was approved by the Ethics Committee.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure study follows ethical guidelines and obtain ethics approval

steps.description: The study was registered in the UMIN Clinical Trials Registry.
steps.searchable_summary: register study in UMIN Clinical Trials Registry

steps.description: Informed consent was obtained from all individual participants included in the study.
Patients with a clinical diagnosis of AC based on symptoms were included, excluding those without allergen test results.
steps.searchable_summary: select participants based on clinical diagnosis and test results
